summary: Rough early‑70 soundboard capture of a primo Dead show featuring a standout China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence.
review: The recording is a raw, early‑70s soundboard tape that sounds a bit rough but remains listenable. The performance shines with Jerry digging into the new China > Rider jam, marking this period as “primo Dead.” One reviewer notes the absence of “Easy Wind,” which tempers the overall enthusiasm.

ai_rating.rationale: Show is praised for strong playing and a notable China > Rider sequence (70% weight), while recording quality is described as rough/typical of the era (30% weight). Mixed comments about missing material lower the overall score slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Typical early‑70s raw sound; described as “rough” but no specific dropouts or balance issues noted. Overall listenable despite the vintage tape character.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑70s set is described as rough yet good, with a strong jam flow highlighted by the China > Rider sequence; overall energy reflects a prime Dead period.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry is noted for digging into the new China > Rider thing and delivering strong guitar work.
